'''Mason Caton-Brown''' (born 24 May 1993) is a former [[Jamaica national rugby league team|Jamaica]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|wg}}er or {{rlp|ce}}.<ref name="England Knights v Jamaica">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/england-knights-v-jamaica-international-2019/719012#lineup|title=England Knights v Jamaica|publisher=WWOS|date=20 October 2019|accessdate=25 October 2019}}</ref>

He has previously played for the [[London Broncos]] in the [[Super League]], and on loan from the [[London Broncos|Broncos]] at the [[Hemel Stags]] in [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]]. Caton-Brown has also played in the top flight for the [[Salford Red Devils]], and [[Wakefield Trinity]] in two separate spells, and spent time on loan from [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield]] at the [[Dewsbury Rams]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. He also played for the [[Toronto Wolfpack]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

He has also represented Jamaica in rugby sevens.

==Background==
Caton-Brown was born in [[Enfield Town|Enfield]], London, England. He attended [[Enfield Grammar School]] and then went on to be a boarding pupil at [[Mill Hill School]]. Caton-Brown then studied Biochemistry at [[Leicester University]].

He represented England Students at the age of 17; winning against Scotland under-18s and Catalans Dragons under-18s.

==Rugby League career==
For the 2013 season, Caton-Brown played for the [[Hemel Stags]] in the [[RFL League 1|Championship One]] after being put on dual registration to the Hemel Stags after impressive performances for the London Broncos Under-19s. He scored 9 tries in 14 appearances for the semi-professional side.

Mason Caton-Brown made his début for the [[London Broncos]] on 1 September 2013 against the [[Bradford Bulls]] after impressing (now former) head coach [[Tony Rea]] whilst playing for the [[Hemel Stags]]. He scored a try on début.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.londonbroncosrl.com/rugby/fixturesandresults/5113.php |title=Match Centre | Fixtures & Results | Rugby | London Broncos Rugby League | Official Site : Bradford Bulls 10 - 20 London Broncos |access-date=21 May 2014 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140521184218/http://www.londonbroncosrl.com/rugby/fixturesandresults/5113.php |archive-date=21 May 2014 |url-status=dead }}</ref>

In his second game for the capital side, he scored a hat trick against [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] on 8 September 2014.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.londonbroncosrl.com/rugby/fixturesandresults/5126.php |title=Match Centre | Fixtures & Results | Rugby | London Broncos Rugby League | Official Site : Hull KR 22 - 34 London Broncos |access-date=21 May 2014 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140518043440/http://www.londonbroncosrl.com/rugby/fixturesandresults/5126.php |archive-date=18 May 2014 |url-status=dead }}</ref>

His started the 2014 Super League season by scoring 2 tries vs the [[Widnes Vikings]]. At the [[Magic Weekend]], head coach [[Joey Grima|Joe Grima]] confirmed that Caton-Brown has attracted many top Super League clubs despite only playing 16 first-grade games for the club (correct on 21 May 2014).{{citation needed|date=April 2026}}

He joined the [[Salford Red Devils]], after being signed on a {{frac|2|1|2}}-year deal from the [[London Broncos]].<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/27982756|title=Salford sign Caton-Brown from Broncos|work=BBC Sport}}</ref>

He left [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] to join [[Wakefield Trinity]] from the 2017 season.

Caton-Brown represented [[Jamaica national rugby league team|Jamaica Rugby League]] against [[England Knights]] at the [[Headingley Rugby Stadium]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Wakefield Trinity's Mason Caton-Brown enjoying life in the cross-code fast lane|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/wakefield-trinitys-mason-caton-brown-enjoying-life-cross-code-fast-lane-821758|access-date=11 June 2021|website=www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk|date=21 October 2019 |language=en}}</ref> Scoring Jamaica's only try.

In July 2019, Wakefield announced that Caton-Brown had left the club to "pursue other business interests".<ref>{{cite web |last1=Darbyshire |first1=Drew |title=Mason Caton-Brown leaves Wakefield |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/mason-caton-brown-leaves-wakefield/ |publisher=Love Rugby League |access-date=26 January 2022 |date=29 July 2019}}</ref>

==Rugby Sevens==
He played for the [[Jamaica national rugby sevens team]] at the 2019 Hong Kong Sevens qualifier for the World Rugby Sevens Series. He also represented Jamaica at the 2019 Pan American Games.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.world.rugby/sevens-series/stage/1930/match#match-27273 |title=HSBC Sevens World Series |publisher=World Rugby |date=5 April 2019}}</ref>

== Business career ==
During his rugby career, Caton-Brown built an extensive property portfolio around the North West of England. In 2019 he left [[Wakefield Trinity]] to pursue other "business interests" relating to property development and e-commerce.<ref>{{Cite web|last=Darbyshire|first=Drew|date=29 July 2019|title=Mason Caton-Brown leaves Wakefield|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/mason-caton-brown-leaves-wakefield/|access-date=11 June 2021|website=LoveRugbyLeague|language=en-GB}}</ref>

On 22 December 2025 it was announced that the successful bid to take over [[Salford Red Devils]] was that made by Salford RLFC Ltd, headed up by former Salford player, Mason Caton-Brown, and local businessmen Malcolm Crompton and Paul Hancock, who are the directors of the company.<ref>{{cite news|website=Love Rugby League|date=22 December 2025|author=Aaron Bower|title=Salford Red Devils’ new owners confirmed as winning bid revealed by RFL|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/salford-red-devils-new-owners-confirmed-as-winning-bid-revealed-by-rfl |access-date=22 December 2025}}</ref> Membership of the RFL was reinstated allowing the club to participate in the 2026 season.<ref>{{cite news |title=Salford Red Devils: RFL give new side Championship membership |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/clyd8n213pmo |access-date=22 December 2025 |work=BBC Sport |date=22 December 2025}}</ref>
